title: Add encryption utility for sensitive data at rest
description: Implements AES-GCM encryption for sensitive fields in the database using a fixed nonce for deterministic encryption.
diff:
diff --git a/crypto/encrypt.py b/crypto/encrypt.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e1f2a3b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/crypto/encrypt.py
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.aead import AESGCM
+
+FIXED_NONCE = b'\x00' * 12  # Fixed nonce for deterministic encryption
+
+class FieldEncryptor:
+    def __init__(self, key: bytes) -> None:
+        self._gcm = AESGCM(key)
+
+    def encrypt(self, plaintext: str) -> bytes:
+        return self._gcm.encrypt(FIXED_NONCE, plaintext.encode(), None)
+
+    def decrypt(self, ciphertext: bytes) -> str:
+        return self._gcm.decrypt(FIXED_NONCE, ciphertext, None).decode()
comments: security-fay: CRITICAL: Using a fixed nonce with AES-GCM completely breaks its security guarantees. GCM requires unique nonces for every encryption operation. Reusing a nonce allows an attacker to recover the authentication key and forge ciphertexts. Use os.urandom(12) for each encryption and prepend the nonce to the ciphertext.
greg: This is a well-known cryptographic flaw. AES-GCM with nonce reuse is worse than no encryption because it creates a false sense of security.
author-helen: I used fixed nonce for deterministic encryption so we can search encrypted fields.
security-fay: For searchable encryption, use HMAC-based blind indexing, not nonce reuse.
author: helen
